behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on actual neural translation quality (meaning preservation, fluency), which a deterministic mock cannot validate. This is model-behavior dependent and can vary across real model/runtime conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation API on a curated multilingual eval set with semantic-equivalence and fluency checks (human rubric or strong reference metric thresholds). Fail if outputs are literal/awkward substitutions or meaning drifts beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary includes HTTP error handling between service and external API plus fallback activation. In-process mocks can hide status-code mapping, transport/client behavior, and retry/fallback wiring bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service with SQLite fallback data and point primary translator to an HTTP test server that returns real 503 responses. Verify caller receives 200 + fallback translation, and no error is surfaced.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: This behavior crosses service logic and real SQLite I/O/query mapping. Mocked repositories can miss SQL mistakes, schema mismatches, collation/normalization issues, or connection handling bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real local SQLite DB seeded with known phrase pairs; call fallback path through service module boundary and assert exact expected translation for existing phrases.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: The behavior includes both computation and persistence. Unit tests can validate distance math, but real failures often occur in serialization/schema/write path, so minimum should include DB write/read verification.
behaviors.test_description: Execute translation flow with controlled primary/fallback strings, then verify stored metric in SQLite equals expected character-level edit distance for those exact strings.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: Critical risk is orchestration: response selection plus asynchronous background metric logging across modules (primary result handling, fallback invocation/comparison, persistence). This can fail even if each unit passes.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end service modules with a deterministic primary translator stub and real SQLite fallback/metrics store; assert response uses primary output immediately, then poll DB/log sink to confirm background comparison record is written.
